title: erica evansii
intro_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", erica evansii benefits are related to its immunomodulatory properties, specifically its essential oils may be effective in modulating some innate immune responses possibly by inhibition of neutrophil migration.
parts: Erica evansii has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, flowers, and roots, which are used in traditional medicine. The leaves of Erica evansii are rich in flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory properties. The flowers of Erica evansii contain phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, which have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The roots of Erica evansii contain triterpenoids, including ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects.
side_effects: Erica evansii can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. Some individuals may experience nausea, dizziness, and headaches after ingesting the plant, which can be exacerbated by large doses or prolonged use. In severe cases, improper use of Erica evansii can lead to gastrointestinal issues, including stomach pain, diarrhea, and vomiting. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using Erica evansii for health benefits to avoid potential side effects.
